title: 第 49 節
en: summer has come, four fingers deep if the winter and ice have come.
summer has come, four fingers deep if the winter and ice have come.
' Thou shalt dig a sixth hole \ two fingers deep if the summer has come, four fingers deep if the winter and ice have come.'
8(14). How far from one another ?
How much is the pace ?
9 (16). *Then thou shalt dig three holes more^ two fingers deep if the summer has come, four fingers deep if the winter and ice have come/ How far from the former six ?
What sort of paces ?
' Such as are taken in walking.' How much are those (three) paces ? ' As much as nine feet.
10(22). 'Then thou shalt draw a furrow all arouni^ with a metal knife.'
^ These six holes contain g6m6z. 'The holes must be dug from the north to the south ' (Comm.)
FARGARD IX. I 25 How far from the holes ?
What sort of paces ?
' Such as are taken in walking.' How much are those (three) paces ?

1 1 (24). * Then thou shalt draw twelve furrows ^ ; three of which thou shalt draw to surround and divide [from the rest] (the first) three holes; three thou shalt draw to surround and divide (the first) six holes ; three thou shalt draw to surround and divide the nine holes ; three thou shalt draw around ihe [three] inferior holes, outside the [six other] holes \ At each of the three times nine feet *, thou shalt place stones as steps to the holes; or potsherds, or stumps *, or clods, or any hard matter ^'

' ' The furrows must be drawn during the day ; they must be drawn with a knife ; they must be drawn with recitation of spells. While drawing the furrows the cleanser recites three Ashem-vohus ('< holiness is the best of all good," &c.), the Fravardn6 (*' I declare myself a worshipper of Mazda, a follower of Zarathujtra, a foe of the fiend," &c.), the Khshndman of Serosh, and the B^ of Serosh ; they must be drawn from the north ' (Comm. ad § 32). The furrow, or kesh, plays a greater part in the Mazdean liturgy than in any other. By means of the furrow, drawn with proper spells, and according to the laws of spiritual war, man either besieges the fiend or intrenches himself against him (cf. Farg. XVII, 5). In the present case the Dnf^, being shut up inside the kesh and thus excluded from the world outside, and being driven back, step by step, by the strength of the holy water and spells, finds at last no place of refuge but hell.
' The nine feet between the holes containing g6m6z and those containing water, the nine feet between the first holes and the furrows, and the nine feet between the last hole and the furrows.
1 26 VENDiDAD.
lb.

12 (31). *Then the man defiled shall walk to the holes ; thou, O Zarathujtra ! shalt stand outside by the furrow, and thou shalt recite, Nemas/fe4 yA drmaiti^ is&kt^ ; and the man defiled shall repeat, Nemasia yd drmaiti^ IzSiki.

13 (35), * The Dn\f becomes weaker and weaker at every one of those words which are a weapon to smite the fiend Angra Mainyu, to smite A^shma of the murderous spear ^ to smite the Mizainya fiends \ to smite all the fiends.

14 (40). * Then thou shalt take for the gdm^z a spoon of brass or of lead. When thou takest a stick with nine knots*, O Spitama Zarathu5tra! to sprinkle (the g6m6z) from that spoon, thou shalt fasten the spoon to the end of the stick.

1 5 (43)- * They shall wash his hands first If his hands be not washed first, he makes his whole body unclean. When he has washed his hands three times, after his hands have been washed, thou shalt sprinkle the forepart of his skull * ; then the Dru^ Nasu rushes In front, between his brows •.
16 (50). Thou shalt sprinkle him in front between the brows ; then the Dru^ Nasu rushes upon the back part of the skull.
then the Dru^ Nasu rushes upon the jaws.
' Yasna XLIX, 10 c. * See Farg. X, 13.
• See Farg. X, 16.
holes and sprinkle the unclean one, without touching him and without going inside the furrows.
• Cf. Farg. VIII, 40-71.
FARGARD IX. 127 'Thou shalt sprinkle the jaws; then the DrvLg' Nasu rushes upon the right ear.
17 (56). * Thou shalt sprinkle the right ear; then the Dru^ Nasu rushes upon the left ear.
zh: 夏季已來臨，若冬季與冰雪已至，則深達四指。
夏季已至，若冬日與冰雪來臨，則深達四指。
「汝當挖掘第六個孔洞，若夏季已至，則深兩指；若冬季與冰凍已至，則深四指。」
我注意到提供的英文原文「How much is the pace?」似乎不是出自穆勒1873年《宗教學導論》的學術文本。這句話在文法上也不完整或不清楚。 請確認： 1. 這是否為正確的英文原文摘錄？ 2. 是否有更完整的上下文或修正後的原文？ 待確認後，我將提供準確的繁體中文翻譯。
9 (16). *那麼你應當再挖三個洞：若夏季已至，深度為兩指寬；若冬季與冰凍已至，深度為四指寬。距離前面六個洞應隔多遠？
什麼樣的步伐？
「如同在行走時所跨出的。」那些（三）步有多長？「有九呎那麼長。」
10(22). 「然後汝應以金屬刀在四周劃一條犁溝。」
^ 這六個孔洞含有g6m6z。「孔洞必須從北向南挖掘」(注疏)
第九法爾格德 第一章 第25節 距離那些洞穴有多遠？
什麼樣的步伐？
「如同行走時所跨越的。」那（三）步有多遠？
然後你應當劃出十二條溝渠；其中三條你應當劃出以圍繞並區分（第一個）三個洞孔；三條你應當劃出以圍繞並區分（第一個）六個洞孔；三條你應當劃出以圍繞並區分九個洞孔；三條你應當劃出以圍繞（三個）下層洞孔，在（其他六個）洞孔之外。在三個九尺的位置各處，你應當在洞孔處放置石塊作為踏腳；或陶片、或樹樁、或土塊、或任何堅硬的物質。

「溝渠必須在白天劃出；必須用刀劃出；必須邊劃邊誦咒文。淨化者在劃溝渠時誦三遍《阿舍姆-沃胡》（「聖潔乃萬善之最」等），《弗拉瓦丁禮文》（「我宣誓為智慧神馬茲達之崇拜者，查拉圖斯特拉之追隨者，惡魔之敵」等），《塞若什之克什納德曼》，以及《塞若什之拜》；溝渠必須從北方劃起」（見《註疏》§32）。溝渠（即 kesh）在瑪茲達教禮儀中的作用，比在任何其他宗教中都更重要。藉由以適當咒文劃出的溝渠，並按照靈性戰爭之法，人類可以圍困惡魔，或者用防禦工事對抗它（參見《法爾加德》第十七卷第五節）。在此情況下，《德烏》被困於 kesh 內部，因而被隔絕於外界，並逐步被聖水與咒文的力量驅趕回退，最終找不到任何避難所，除了地獄。
「含有葛莫茲（g6m6z）的洞穴與含有水的洞穴之間相隔九英尺，第一批洞穴與水渠之間相隔九英尺，最後一個洞穴與水渠之間相隔九英尺。」
梵達德（Vendidad）。

12（31）。*然則被污穢之人當走向孔洞；汝，喔，琐罗亚斯德啊！當立於田埂之外，而汝當誦曰「Nemasfé yd drmaiti^ iSzkt^」；被污穢之人當重複誦曰「Nemasia yd drmaiti^ IzSiki」。
13 (35), * 當每一個詞語成為武器去擊倒惡魔安格拉‧邁紐（Angra Mainyu）、去擊倒持殺人之矛的阿什瑪（A^shma）、去擊倒米紮尼亞的惡魔、去擊倒所有惡魔時，德律克（Dn\f）就變得越來越虛弱。
14 (40). * 其後汝當為聖火取一把銅質或鉛質之匙。當汝取一根有九個結點*之枝條，喔，光明者瑣羅亞斯德啊！欲以彼匙灑聖火，汝當將此匙繫於枝條之末端。
他們應先洗淨其雙手。若其雙手未先洗淨，他將使其整個身體不潔。當他洗淨雙手三次後，雙手既已洗淨，汝應灑水於其頭顱前部*；則 Dru^ Nasu 則衝向前來，位於其兩眉之間‧。
16 (50). 汝應向其額間兩眉之處灑淨；爾時毒鬼那蘇（Dru^ Nasu）乃撲向頭骨之後部。
然後乾屍惡魔（Dru^ Nasu）撲向顎部。
《雅斯那經》（Yasna）第四十九卷，第十節丙。*參見《法爾迦德》（Farg.）第十卷，第十三節。
• 見《法爾加德》第十卷第16節。
洞孔中灑向不潔淨的人，既不觸及其身，也不踏入田壟之內。
• 參見《法爾剛經》第八卷第40-71節。
FARGARD IX. 127「汝應灑淨其顎；其後惡魔之屍體穢氣（Nasu）撲向右耳。」
17 (56). * 汝應灑淨右耳；爾後屍穢（Dru^ Nasu）撲向左耳。
